title: tsuga yunnanensis subsp. dura
intro: Tsuga yunnanensis subsp. dura, also known as the Yunnan fir, is a subspecies of evergreen coniferous tree native to the mountainous regions of Yunnan Province in China. The bark and needles of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Tsuga yunnanensis subsp. dura are numerous, including its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant's bark and needles are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.
